channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: White House blocks AP reporter from Trump-Modi news conference because of Gulf of Mexico fight
item.description: The White House blocked an Associated Press journalist from covering a news conference with two major world leaders Thursday, upping the stakes in a disagreement between the news agency and the Trump administration over AP's style decision to stick with the name “Gulf of Mexico” for the body of wate
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Order to drop New York Mayor Adams' case roils Justice Department as high-ranking officials resign
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Manhattan’s top federal prosecutor, Danielle Sassoon, and five high-ranking Justice Department officials resigned Thursday after she refused an order to drop corruption charges against New York City Mayor Eric Adams — a stunning escalation in a dayslong standoff over the Trump admini
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Suspended Hillsborough Democrat defends online posts some say are antisemitic
item.description: The Hillsborough County Democratic Executive Committee has suspended a member until January 2027 over posts online and wording in email messages.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump team digs in, finds millions of wasted dollars at EPA, HUD with DOGE help
item.description: EPA administrator Lee Zeldin said his team canceled a $50 million Biden-era environmental justice grant to a group that believes "climate justice travels through a Free Palestine.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: RFK Jr. sworn in as Health and Human Services secretary
item.description: Hours after being confirmed in the Senate, Robert F. Kennedy Jr. was sworn in as Health and Human Services secretary alongside his family in the Oval Office.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump seems to question well-known part of Sen. Mitch McConnell's life
item.description: President Donald Trump seemed to question a well-documented part of Sen. Mitch McConnell's life — his childhood battle with polio — after the Kentucky Republican opposed vaccine skeptic Robert F. Kennedy Jr.'s confirmation Thursday as the nation's top health official.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: EPA chief says he will seek return of $20 billion in clean-energy grants awarded by Biden
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — In a major reversal, the new head of the Environmental Protection Agency said he will try to rescind $20 billion in grants awarded by the Biden administration for climate and clean-energy projects.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW DALY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: North Carolina school board member rips ‘mediocre White men’ in rant against DEI critics
item.description: Wake County, North Carolina, Schools board member Sam Hershey made waves after referring to critics of DEI as potentially "mediocre White men" among other comments.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Georgetown probes student event headlined by convicted terrorist; advocates demand it be canceled
item.description: Georgetown is facing demands from a pro-Jewish legal advocacy group that has said the university must cancel an event headlined by a convicted member of a Palestinian terror group.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Concerns over mass deportations lead to ‘Know Your Rights‘ discussion in Brooklyn Center
item.description: Amid concerns over the Trump Administration’s plans for mass deportations, nearly three dozen people who live in Brooklyn Park and Brooklyn Center attended a panel discussion Wednesday night to ask questions about how to respond if ICE shows up at a home or workplace, and also asked about the role o
item.dc:creator: Sarah Thamer, MPR News

item.title: Senate panel advances nomination of Kash Patel, Trump's pick to lead the FBI
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Senate Judiciary Committee voted along party lines on Thursday to advance the nomination of Kash Patel, Donald Trump's pick for FBI director, pushing past Democratic concerns that he would operate as a loyalist for the president and target perceived adversaries of the White Hou
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump’s buyout offer accepted by about 77,000 federal workers, White House says
item.description: The number amounts to less than 4% of the federal workforce, short of the White House's goal of 5% to 10%.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, Fox 5

item.title: CA, 18 other states' AGs celebrate injunction against Trump's citizenship order
item.description: LOS ANGELES (KTLA)—California Attorney General Rob Bonta and 18 other state attorneys general have issued a statement after another judge blocked President Donald Trump's executive order seeking to terminate birthright citizenship.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: State Department halts plan to buy $400M worth of armored vehicles from Musk's Tesla
item.description: The State Department had been in talks with Elon Musk’s Tesla company to buy armored electric vehicles, but the plans have been put on hold by the Trump administration after reports emerged about a potential $400 million purchase.
item.dc:creator: Adriana Gomez Licon, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Sen. Ted Cruz’s list of ’woke’ science includes self-driving cars and solar eclipses
item.description: Sen. Ted Cruz, R-Texas, says the National Science Foundation has given money to thousands of “woke DEI” studies. Researchers say that’s misrepresenting science and disparaging important research.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Lambert, MPR News

item.title: Could Kamala Harris be the next California governor? Here's how she's polling
item.description: Kamala Harris holds a substantial lead over other potential candidates to succeed California Gov. Gavin Newsom in 2026.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, NewsNation

item.title: Hard-liners, leadership strike deal on budget resolution ahead of key vote
item.description: Hard-line conservatives and House GOP leadership struck an agreement on the conference’s budget resolution shortly before a key vote Thursday, putting the measure on a path to advance out of committee if it holds. According to House Freedom Caucus Chair Andy Harris (R-Md.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Robert F. Kennedy Jr. confirmed as Trump's health secretary after close Senate vote
item.description: The Senate on Thursday confirmed Robert F. Kennedy Jr. as President Donald Trump’s health secretary, putting the prominent vaccine skeptic in control of $1.7 trillion in federal spending, vaccine recommendations and food safety as well as health insurance programs for roughly half the country.
item.dc:creator: AMANDA SEITZ Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Senate confirms RFK Jr. as Health secretary; McConnell lone GOP dissenter
item.description: Longtime vaccine critic Robert F. Kennedy Jr. is now the nation’s top health official, after the Senate on Thursday voted almost entirely on party lines to confirm him atop a department of nearly 100,000 employees that runs 13 agencies.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Minnesota’s Tina Smith won't seek reelection to Senate
item.description: Sen. Tina Smith, who has represented Minnesota in the U.S. Senate since 2018, won’t try for another six-year term next year. An open seat could set off a scramble in both parties ahead of the midterm election.
item.dc:creator: Brian Bakst, MPR News

item.title: Watch live: House panel convenes hearing on USAID amid DOGE overhaul
item.description: The House Foreign Affairs Committee is holding a hearing Thursday morning looking into what GOP leadership has called the "betrayal" of the U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID).
item.dc:creator: The Hill Staff, NewsNation

item.title: Trump’s buyout offer accepted by about 77,000 federal workers
item.description: The number amounts to less than 4% of the federal workforce, short of the White House's goal of 5 to 10%.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: DOJ sues NY Gov. Hochul, AG James over immigration policies
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump's newly installed attorney general, Pam Bondi, went after New York leaders Wednesday over the state's immigration policies, announcing a lawsuit in the latest effort by the Republican administration to carry out the president's hardline immigration campaign p
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Republicans advance anti-abortion legislation during control of Minnesota House
item.description: Republicans have control of the Minnesota House at least through a special election in March, giving them a chance to advance abortion legislation that would have a tougher path in a tied chamber.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Trump upends U.S. policy on Ukraine and says he and Putin have agreed to begin talks on ending the war
item.description: President Donald Trump upended three years of U.S. policy toward Ukraine on Wednesday, saying that he and Russian President Vladimir Putin had agreed to begin negotiations on ending the war following a dramatic prisoner swap.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Tampa Bay lawmaker files bill to allow state funds to be invested in Bitcoin
item.description: A Tampa Bay lawmaker filed a bill on Friday that would allow Florida's chief financial officer to invest state funds in Bitcoin.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Accettulla, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump says he'll sign order promoting plastic straws
item.description: President Donald Trump on Friday said he plans to sign an executive order promoting plastic straws after his predecessor sought to phase out single-use plastics from government operations.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5

item.title: Members of Congress denied access to Department of Education
item.description: Multiple members of Congress were denied entry to the Department of Education on Friday, according to videos and social media posts from lawmakers.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5
